mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of months where UNRATE increases by over 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive 60-day return direction in at least 50% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing activity that precedes negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during late-cycle phases yields positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level below 2.5 percent establishes an accommodative monetary baseline that reduces equity valuation pressure and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling total nonfarm payrolls confirms a deteriorating labor market that erodes consumer spending capacity and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The UNRATE-PAYEMS divergence signal produces positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 50% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly translates to increased corporate revenue expectations and equity price appreciation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 against 20-day forward returns remains below 0.01 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle amplifies recession fears and triggers immediate risk-off selling, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes during tightening cycles yield positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances.
